problem: Who was awarded the Oceanography Society's Jerlov Award in 2018?

response.answer:
The Oceanography Society awarded the Jerlov Award in 2018 to Dr. Annick Bricaud. She was recognized for her contributions to advancing knowledge of the nature and consequences of light in the ocean, including her groundbreaking work in ocean optics, seawater optical properties, bio-optical relationships, and satellite ocean color algorithms. Dr. Bricaud is a senior research scientist at the Laboratoire d’Océanographie de Villefranche (LOV), CNRS – Sorbonne Université, France.

Source: https://2018.oceanopticsconference.org/jerlov-award/

problem: What's the name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts?

response.answer: The women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, is Radcliffe College. It was founded in 1879 and served as a female coordinate institution for Harvard University before being fully incorporated into Harvard in 1999.

problem: In whose honor was the Leipzig 1877 tournament organized?

response.answer: The Leipzig 1877 chess tournament was organized in honor of Adolf Anderssen. It was held to celebrate Anderssen's 50th anniversary of learning chess.

problem: According to Karl Küchler, what did Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture depict, which was made for her villa Achilleion at Corfu?

response.answer: According to Karl Küchler, Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture at her villa Achilleion at Corfu depicted Achilles mourning the death of Patroclus. It was a life-size marble sculpture by Ludwig Schwanthaler, based on an antique model. Küchler highlights that Sisi identified strongly with Achilles' grief, seeing parallels to her own personal losses and melancholic disposition, making the sculpture a central symbol of the Achilleion and her emotional world.

problem: How much money, in euros, was the surgeon held responsible for Stella Obasanjo's death ordered to pay her son?

response.answer:
The surgeon held responsible for Stella Obasanjo's death was ordered to pay 120,000 euros to her son, Olumuyiwa Obasanjo. This was part of the court's ruling after sentencing the surgeon to one year imprisonment and suspending his professional license for three years. The surgeon was found to have caused serious and reckless harm during the operation that led to Stella Obasanjo's death.

Source: Vanguard News article (https://www.vanguardngr.com/2009/09/stella-obasanjos-killer-doctor-jailed/)

problem: Who appointed the Chief Justice of India, Mirza Hameedullah Beg, in 1977?

response.answer: Mirza Hameedullah Beg was appointed as the Chief Justice of India on 29 January 1977 by Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed, who was the President of India at that time. The appointment was made by the Indira Gandhi government and was controversial because it superseded senior judges, including H. R. Khanna.

problem: What is the name of the former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971?

response.answer: The former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971 is Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir. She was a flight attendant with Icelandic Airlines from 1962 to 1971 before entering politics and later serving as Prime Minister of Iceland from 2009 to 2013. Jóhann Hafstein was also a former Prime Minister of Iceland but there is no evidence that he worked as cabin crew.

problem: To whom did Mehbooba Mufti Sayed contest the 2019 Lok Sabha elections and lose?

response.answer: Mehbooba Mufti contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections from the Anantnag seat and lost to Hasnain Masoodi of the National Conference.

problem: What year did the Lego part with ID gal56 first release?

response.answer: The Lego part with ID gal56 was first released in the year 2002. This part is a Galidor accessory used by the character Euripides and was introduced in 2002 according to the Brickipedia Fandom page.

problem: In which year did the Japanese scientist Koichi Mizushima receive the Kato Memorial Prize?

response.answer: The Japanese scientist Koichi Mizushima received the Kato Memorial Prize in the year 1999.

problem: In which year did Melbourne's Monash Gallery of Art (MGA) rebrand and become the Museum of Australian Photography (MAPh)?

response.answer: Melbourne's Monash Gallery of Art (MGA) rebranded and became the Museum of Australian Photography (MAPh) in the year 2023, specifically in March 2023.

problem: Who requested the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) implement a 900 sq mi (2,300 km2) temporary flight restriction zone over the operations areas of the Deepwater Horizon?

response.answer: The U.S. Coast Guard requested the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to implement the 900 sq mi (2,300 km2) temporary flight restriction zone over the operations areas of the Deepwater Horizon spill. This was done to prevent civilian air traffic from interfering with aircraft assisting the response effort.

problem: What signature piece of the MOBA did Scott Wilson discover on the curb between two trash cans?
answer: Lucy in the Field with Flowers

response.answer: Scott Wilson discovered the signature piece of the Museum of Bad Art (MOBA) on the curb between two trash cans. He pulled the gallery's first piece out of a trash heap and dubbed it "Lucy in the Field with Flowers." This piece became the founding artwork of the MOBA collection, which has since grown to include hundreds of other pieces. The MOBA was originally housed in the Dedham Community Theater and has now moved to the Dorchester Brewing Company.

problem: What player scored all the conversions for Spain in the rugby match between Spain and Romania that was part of the 2022 Rugby Europe Championship on February 27, 2022?

response.answer: The player who scored all the conversions for Spain in the rugby match between Spain and Romania on February 27, 2022, as part of the 2022 Rugby Europe Championship, was Manuel Ordas.
